instruction: Design a TypeScript monorepo setup using npm workspaces with shared types, a React frontend package, an Express API package, and a shared utilities library. Show the project structure and key configuration files.
output:
<think>
Monorepo setup is one of the most practical real-world challenges in JS/TS. npm workspaces (built into npm 7+) is the simplest approach — no extra tooling like Turborepo or Lerna needed for basic cases. The key decisions: project structure (packages/ directory), shared tsconfig (extends base), cross-package references (TypeScript project references for type checking, workspace:* protocol for dependencies). I need to think about: build order (shared types → utils → api/frontend), how the frontend imports from shared packages (needs proper exports field in package.json), and dev workflow (running api and frontend concurrently). A common gotcha: TypeScript path aliases that work in one package but not another — the solution is proper package.json exports with types field. I should also show how to handle the case where the shared package needs to be built before consumers can use it, or use TypeScript project references for live development.
</think>

```
# Project Structure
my-app/
├── package.json              # Root workspace config
├── tsconfig.base.json        # Shared TS config
├── packages/
│   ├── types/                # @my-app/types
│   │   ├── package.json
│   │   ├── tsconfig.json
│   │   └── src/
│   │       └── index.ts
│   ├── utils/                # @my-app/utils
│   │   ├── package.json
│   │   ├── tsconfig.json
│   │   └── src/
│   │       └── index.ts
│   ├── api/                  # @my-app/api
│   │   ├── package.json
│   │   ├── tsconfig.json
│   │   └── src/
│   │       └── index.ts
│   └── frontend/             # @my-app/frontend
│       ├── package.json
│       ├── tsconfig.json
│       └── src/
│           ├── App.tsx
│           └── index.tsx
```

```json
// Root package.json
{
  "name": "my-app",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["packages/*"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "npm run build --workspaces",
    "dev": "concurrently \"npm run dev -w @my-app/api\" \"npm run dev -w @my-app/frontend\"",
    "typecheck": "tsc --build packages/*/tsconfig.json",
    "clean": "rm -rf packages/*/dist"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "concurrently": "^8.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.4.0"
  }
}
```

```json
// tsconfig.base.json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "Node16",
    "moduleResolution": "Node16",
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "composite": true
  }
}
```

```json
// packages/types/package.json
{
  "name": "@my-app/types",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.js",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc"
  }
}
```

```typescript
// packages/types/src/index.ts
export interface User {
  id: string;
  email: string;
  name: string;
  role: 'admin' | 'user' | 'viewer';
  createdAt: string;
}

export interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  meta?: {
    page: number;
    total: number;
    perPage: number;
  };
}

export interface ApiError {
  code: string;
  message: string;
  details?: Record<string, string[]>;
}

export type Result<T> =
  | { ok: true; data: T }
  | { ok: false; error: ApiError };
```

```json
// packages/utils/package.json
{
  "name": "@my-app/utils",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "scripts": { "build": "tsc" },
  "dependencies": {
    "@my-app/types": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

```typescript
// packages/utils/src/index.ts
import type { ApiError, Result } from '@my-app/types';

export function ok<T>(data: T): Result<T> {
  return { ok: true, data };
}

export function err<T>(code: string, message: string): Result<T> {
  return { ok: false, error: { code, message } };
}

export function formatDate(iso: string): string {
  return new Date(iso).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
    year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric',
  });
}

export function paginate<T>(items: T[], page: number, perPage: number) {
  const start = (page - 1) * perPage;
  return {
    data: items.slice(start, start + perPage),
    meta: { page, total: items.length, perPage },
  };
}
```

```json
// packages/api/package.json
{
  "name": "@my-app/api",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "dev": "tsx watch src/index.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@my-app/types": "workspace:*",
    "@my-app/utils": "workspace:*",
    "express": "^4.18.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/express": "^4.17.0",
    "tsx": "^4.0.0"
  }
}
```

```typescript
// packages/api/src/index.ts
import express from 'express';
import type { User, ApiResponse } from '@my-app/types';
import { ok, paginate } from '@my-app/utils';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const users: User[] = [
  { id: '1', email: 'alice@example.com', name: 'Alice', role: 'admin', createdAt: new Date().toISOString() },
];

app.get('/api/users', (req, res) => {
  const page = Number(req.query.page) || 1;
  const result = paginate(users, page, 20);
  const response: ApiResponse<User[]> = result;
  res.json(response);
});

app.listen(3001, () => console.log('API on :3001'));
```

instruction: Build a type-safe API client in TypeScript that auto-generates request functions from an API schema, handles authentication, retries, and provides discriminated union responses for error handling.
output:
<think>
Type-safe API clients are where TypeScript really shines. The goal: define the API schema once (as TypeScript types), and get fully typed request functions with autocomplete. I'll use a generic approach where each endpoint is defined with its method, path, request body type, and response type. The client should handle: auth token injection (via interceptor), automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient errors, and return discriminated union types so callers can narrow with `if (result.ok)`. Key design decisions: use fetch as the transport (works in Node and browser), make the schema definition as ergonomic as possible with mapped types, and ensure the error type includes the HTTP status and parsed body for API errors. Retries should only happen on 5xx and network errors, never on 4xx (client errors). The auth should support both static tokens and async token providers (for refresh tokens).
</think>

```typescript
// --- API Schema Definition ---

interface EndpointDef {
  method: 'GET' | 'POST' | 'PUT' | 'DELETE' | 'PATCH';
  path: string;
  body?: unknown;
  query?: Record<string, string | number>;
  response: unknown;
}

// Define your API schema as a type map
interface ApiSchema {
  'getUsers': {
    method: 'GET';
    path: '/api/users';
    query: { page?: number; limit?: number };
    response: { users: User[]; total: number };
  };
  'getUser': {
    method: 'GET';
    path: '/api/users/:id';
    response: User;
  };
  'createUser': {
    method: 'POST';
    path: '/api/users';
    body: { email: string; name: string; role: string };
    response: User;
  };
  'updateUser': {
    method: 'PUT';
    path: '/api/users/:id';
    body: Partial<{ email: string; name: string; role: string }>;
    response: User;
  };
  'deleteUser': {
    method: 'DELETE';
    path: '/api/users/:id';
    response: { deleted: boolean };
  };
}

interface User {
  id: string;
  email: string;
  name: string;
  role: string;
}

// --- Discriminated Union Result ---

type ApiResult<T> =
  | { ok: true; status: number; data: T }
  | { ok: false; status: number; error: ApiErrorBody }
  | { ok: false; status: 0; error: { code: 'NETWORK_ERROR'; message: string } };

interface ApiErrorBody {
  code: string;
  message: string;
  details?: Record<string, string[]>;
}

// --- Client Configuration ---

interface ClientConfig {
  baseUrl: string;
  auth?: (() => Promise<string>) | string;
  retries?: number;
  retryDelayMs?: number;
  timeout?: number;
}

// --- Path parameter extraction ---

type ExtractPathParams<T extends string> =
  T extends `${infer _Start}:${infer Param}/${infer Rest}`
    ? { [K in Param | keyof ExtractPathParams<`/${Rest}`>]: string }
    : T extends `${infer _Start}:${infer Param}`
      ? { [K in Param]: string }
      : Record<string, never>;

type HasPathParams<T extends string> =
  T extends `${string}:${string}` ? true : false;

// --- Request options builder ---

type RequestOptions<E extends EndpointDef> =
  & (E extends { body: infer B } ? { body: B } : {})
  & (E extends { query: infer Q } ? { query?: Q } : {})
  & (HasPathParams<E['path']> extends true ? { params: ExtractPathParams<E['path']> } : {});

// --- Client Factory ---

function createApiClient(config: ClientConfig) {
  const { baseUrl, retries = 2, retryDelayMs = 500, timeout = 10000 } = config;

  async function getAuthHeader(): Promise<Record<string, string>> {
    if (!config.auth) return {};
    const token = typeof config.auth === 'string'
      ? config.auth
      : await config.auth();
    return { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` };
  }

  function buildUrl(path: string, params?: Record<string, string>, query?: Record<string, any>): string {
    let url = path;
    if (params) {
      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {
        url = url.replace(`:${key}`, encodeURIComponent(value));
      }
    }
    const base = baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '') + url;
    if (query) {
      const qs = new URLSearchParams();
      for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(query)) {
        if (v !== undefined) qs.set(k, String(v));
      }
      const qsStr = qs.toString();
      return qsStr ? `${base}?${qsStr}` : base;
    }
    return base;
  }

  async function request<E extends EndpointDef>(
    method: E['method'],
    path: E['path'],
    options: any = {},
  ): Promise<ApiResult<E['response']>> {
    const url = buildUrl(path, options.params, options.query);
    const auth = await getAuthHeader();

    const fetchOptions: RequestInit = {
      method,
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        ...auth,
      },
      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(timeout),
    };

    if (options.body) {
      fetchOptions.body = JSON.stringify(options.body);
    }

    for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= retries; attempt++) {
      try {
        const res = await fetch(url, fetchOptions);

        if (res.ok) {
          const data = await res.json();
          return { ok: true, status: res.status, data };
        }

        // Don't retry client errors
        if (res.status < 500) {
          const error = await res.json().catch(() => ({
            code: 'UNKNOWN', message: res.statusText,
          }));
          return { ok: false, status: res.status, error };
        }

        // Server error — retry if attempts remain
        if (attempt === retries) {
          const error = await res.json().catch(() => ({
            code: 'SERVER_ERROR', message: `${res.status} ${res.statusText}`,
          }));
          return { ok: false, status: res.status, error };
        }

        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, retryDelayMs * (attempt + 1)));
      } catch (err) {
        if (attempt === retries) {
          return {
            ok: false, status: 0,
            error: { code: 'NETWORK_ERROR', message: String(err) },
          };
        }
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, retryDelayMs * (attempt + 1)));
      }
    }

    // Unreachable, but TypeScript needs it
    return { ok: false, status: 0, error: { code: 'NETWORK_ERROR', message: 'exhausted retries' } };
  }

  // Build typed endpoint functions
  function endpoint<K extends keyof ApiSchema>(name: K) {
    type E = ApiSchema[K];
    return (options: RequestOptions<E>): Promise<ApiResult<E['response']>> => {
      const schema = schemaMap[name];
      return request<E>(schema.method as any, schema.path as any, options);
    };
  }

  // Schema map for runtime access
  const schemaMap: Record<keyof ApiSchema, { method: string; path: string }> = {
    getUsers:   { method: 'GET', path: '/api/users' },
    getUser:    { method: 'GET', path: '/api/users/:id' },
    createUser: { method: 'POST', path: '/api/users' },
    updateUser: { method: 'PUT', path: '/api/users/:id' },
    deleteUser: { method: 'DELETE', path: '/api/users/:id' },
  };

  return {
    getUsers:   endpoint('getUsers'),
    getUser:    endpoint('getUser'),
    createUser: endpoint('createUser'),
    updateUser: endpoint('updateUser'),
    deleteUser: endpoint('deleteUser'),
  };
}

// --- Usage ---

const api = createApiClient({
  baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
  auth: async () => localStorage.getItem('token') ?? '',
  retries: 2,
});

async function demo() {
  const result = await api.getUsers({ query: { page: 1, limit: 20 } });
  if (result.ok) {
    console.log(result.data.users); // Fully typed: User[]
  } else {
    console.error(result.error.code, result.error.message);
  }

  const user = await api.createUser({
    body: { email: 'bob@test.com', name: 'Bob', role: 'user' },
  });
  if (user.ok) {
    console.log(user.data.id); // Typed as string
  }
}
```

instruction: Implement a robust WebSocket reconnection manager in TypeScript that handles exponential backoff, connection state tracking, message queuing during disconnection, and heartbeat-based health checks.
output:
<think>
WebSocket reconnection is critical for real-world apps but surprisingly tricky. The main challenges: detecting disconnections (WebSocket close events are unreliable, especially for network drops), queuing messages during downtime, and reconnecting without losing state. I need several components: exponential backoff with jitter for reconnection attempts, a heartbeat system (ping/pong at the application level since browser WebSocket API doesn't expose ping frames), message queue that buffers during disconnection and flushes on reconnect, and proper state machine (connecting → connected → disconnecting → disconnected → reconnecting). For the heartbeat: send a ping every N seconds, if no pong received within M seconds, consider the connection dead and reconnect. The backoff should have a max delay and reset on successful connection. Message queue needs a max size to prevent memory issues. I should also handle the case where the page is backgrounded (visibility API) to pause reconnection attempts.
</think>

```typescript
type ConnectionState = 'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'reconnecting';

interface WSConfig {
  url: string;
  protocols?: string[];
  maxReconnectAttempts: number;
  baseDelayMs: number;
  maxDelayMs: number;
  heartbeatIntervalMs: number;
  heartbeatTimeoutMs: number;
  maxQueueSize: number;
  onMessage?: (data: any) => void;
  onStateChange?: (state: ConnectionState) => void;
  onError?: (error: Event) => void;
}

const DEFAULT_CONFIG: Partial<WSConfig> = {
  maxReconnectAttempts: 10,
  baseDelayMs: 1000,
  maxDelayMs: 30000,
  heartbeatIntervalMs: 25000,
  heartbeatTimeoutMs: 10000,
  maxQueueSize: 100,
};

class WebSocketManager {
  private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
  private config: WSConfig;
  private state: ConnectionState = 'disconnected';
  private reconnectAttempts = 0;
  private messageQueue: Array<string | ArrayBuffer> = [];
  private heartbeatTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
  private heartbeatTimeout: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
  private reconnectTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
  private intentionalClose = false;
  private visibilityHandler: (() => void) | null = null;

  constructor(config: Partial<WSConfig> & Pick<WSConfig, 'url'>) {
    this.config = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...config } as WSConfig;
  }

  get connectionState(): ConnectionState {
    return this.state;
  }

  get queuedMessageCount(): number {
    return this.messageQueue.length;
  }

  connect(): void {
    if (this.state === 'connected' || this.state === 'connecting') {
      return;
    }

    this.intentionalClose = false;
    this.setState('connecting');
    this.setupVisibilityHandler();

    try {
      this.ws = new WebSocket(this.config.url, this.config.protocols);
    } catch (e) {
      this.handleDisconnect();
      return;
    }

    this.ws.onopen = () => {
      this.setState('connected');
      this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
      this.startHeartbeat();
      this.flushQueue();
    };

    this.ws.onmessage = (event) => {
      // Handle heartbeat pong
      if (event.data === '__pong__') {
        this.clearHeartbeatTimeout();
        return;
      }

      try {
        const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
        this.config.onMessage?.(data);
      } catch {
        this.config.onMessage?.(event.data);
      }
    };

    this.ws.onclose = (event) => {
      this.stopHeartbeat();
      if (!this.intentionalClose) {
        console.log(`[ws] Connection closed: code=${event.code} reason=${event.reason}`);
        this.handleDisconnect();
      } else {
        this.setState('disconnected');
      }
    };

    this.ws.onerror = (event) => {
      this.config.onError?.(event);
    };
  }

  disconnect(): void {
    this.intentionalClose = true;
    this.clearTimers();
    this.removeVisibilityHandler();

    if (this.ws) {
      this.ws.close(1000, 'Client disconnect');
      this.ws = null;
    }

    this.setState('disconnected');
  }

  send(data: object | string): boolean {
    const payload = typeof data === 'string' ? data : JSON.stringify(data);

    if (this.state === 'connected' && this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      this.ws.send(payload);
      return true;
    }

    // Queue for later delivery
    if (this.messageQueue.length < this.config.maxQueueSize) {
      this.messageQueue.push(payload);
      console.log(`[ws] Queued message (${this.messageQueue.length} pending)`);
      return true;
    }

    console.warn('[ws] Message queue full, dropping message');
    return false;
  }

  private setState(state: ConnectionState): void {
    if (this.state !== state) {
      const prev = this.state;
      this.state = state;
      console.log(`[ws] State: ${prev} -> ${state}`);
      this.config.onStateChange?.(state);
    }
  }

  private handleDisconnect(): void {
    this.ws = null;
    this.stopHeartbeat();

    if (this.reconnectAttempts >= this.config.maxReconnectAttempts) {
      console.error(`[ws] Max reconnect attempts (${this.config.maxReconnectAttempts}) reached`);
      this.setState('disconnected');
      return;
    }

    this.setState('reconnecting');
    const delay = this.getBackoffDelay();
    console.log(`[ws] Reconnecting in ${delay}ms (attempt ${this.reconnectAttempts + 1})`);

    this.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
      this.reconnectAttempts++;
      this.connect();
    }, delay);
  }

  private getBackoffDelay(): number {
    const exponential = this.config.baseDelayMs * Math.pow(2, this.reconnectAttempts);
    const capped = Math.min(exponential, this.config.maxDelayMs);
    // Add jitter: +-25%
    const jitter = capped * 0.25 * (Math.random() * 2 - 1);
    return Math.round(capped + jitter);
  }

  private flushQueue(): void {
    if (this.messageQueue.length === 0) return;
    console.log(`[ws] Flushing ${this.messageQueue.length} queued messages`);

    while (this.messageQueue.length > 0) {
      const msg = this.messageQueue.shift()!;
      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
        this.ws.send(msg);
      } else {
        this.messageQueue.unshift(msg);
        break;
      }
    }
  }

  private startHeartbeat(): void {
    this.stopHeartbeat();
    this.heartbeatTimer = setInterval(() => {
      if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
        this.ws.send('__ping__');
        this.heartbeatTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
          console.warn('[ws] Heartbeat timeout — connection dead');
          this.ws?.close(4000, 'Heartbeat timeout');
        }, this.config.heartbeatTimeoutMs);
      }
    }, this.config.heartbeatIntervalMs);
  }

  private stopHeartbeat(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimer) clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimer);
    this.clearHeartbeatTimeout();
    this.heartbeatTimer = null;
  }

  private clearHeartbeatTimeout(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
      clearTimeout(this.heartbeatTimeout);
      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
    }
  }

  private clearTimers(): void {
    this.stopHeartbeat();
    if (this.reconnectTimer) {
      clearTimeout(this.reconnectTimer);
      this.reconnectTimer = null;
    }
  }

  private setupVisibilityHandler(): void {
    if (typeof document === 'undefined') return;
    this.visibilityHandler = () => {
      if (document.visibilityState === 'visible' && this.state === 'disconnected') {
        console.log('[ws] Tab visible, attempting reconnect');
        this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
        this.connect();
      }
    };
    document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', this.visibilityHandler);
  }

  private removeVisibilityHandler(): void {
    if (this.visibilityHandler) {
      document.removeEventListener('visibilitychange', this.visibilityHandler);
      this.visibilityHandler = null;
    }
  }
}

// --- Usage ---

const ws = new WebSocketManager({
  url: 'wss://api.example.com/ws',
  onMessage: (data) => console.log('Received:', data),
  onStateChange: (state) => {
    document.getElementById('ws-status')!.textContent = state;
  },
  maxReconnectAttempts: 15,
  heartbeatIntervalMs: 20000,
});

ws.connect();

// Messages sent while disconnected are queued and flushed on reconnect
ws.send({ type: 'subscribe', channel: 'updates' });
```

instruction: Build a Server-Side Rendering (SSR) hydration system in TypeScript/React that handles the mismatch between server-rendered HTML and client-side React, with proper error boundaries and loading states.
output:
SSR hydration is one of the trickiest parts of React development. The core problem: the server renders HTML, sends it to the client, and React needs to "hydrate" (attach event handlers to existing DOM) rather than re-render. If the HTML doesn't match what React would render, you get hydration errors. Common causes: browser-only APIs (window, localStorage), timestamps, random IDs, and conditional rendering based on client state. I need several components: a HydrationProvider that tracks hydration state, a ClientOnly wrapper for browser-dependent content, an error boundary that catches hydration failures gracefully, and a useHydrated hook. The key insight: during hydration, render exactly what the server rendered. After hydration, you can render client-specific content. React 18's hydrateRoot helps with concurrent features but the mismatch problem remains. I should also handle the case where hydration fails — fall back to client-side rendering instead of a blank screen. ```tsx import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect, useMemo, useCallback, type ReactNode, type ErrorInfo, } from 'react'; import { hydrateRoot, createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; // --- Hydration Context --- interface HydrationState { isHydrated: boolean; isServer: boolean; hydrationErrors: HydrationError[]; } interface HydrationError { componentName: string; error: Error; timestamp: number; } const HydrationContext = createContext({ isHydrated: false, isServer: typeof window === 'undefined', hydrationErrors: [], }); export function useHydrated(): boolean { return useContext(HydrationContext).isHydrated; } export function useIsServer(): boolean { return useContext(HydrationContext).isServer; } // --- Hydration Provider --- export function HydrationProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) { const [isHydrated, setIsHydrated] = useState(false); const [hydrationErrors, setHydrationErrors] = useState([]); const isServer = typeof window === 'undefined'; useEffect(() => { // This effect only runs on the client, after hydration setIsHydrated(true); }, []); const state = useMemo( () => ({ isHydrated, isServer, hydrationErrors }), [isHydrated, isServer, hydrationErrors] ); return ( {children} ); } // --- ClientOnly: Renders children only after hydration --- interface ClientOnlyProps { children: ReactNode; fallback?: ReactNode; } export function ClientOnly({ children, fallback = null }: ClientOnlyProps) { const isHydrated = useHydrated(); if (!isHydrated) { return <>{fallback}; } return <>{children}; } // --- Hydration-safe value hook --- export function useHydrationSafe(serverValue: T, clientValue: T): T { const isHydrated = useHydrated(); return isHydrated ? clientValue : serverValue; } // Example: timestamps, random IDs, window dimensions export function useWindowSize() { const isHydrated = useHydrated(); const [size, setSize] = useState({ width: 0, height: 0 }); useEffect(() => { if (!isHydrated) return; const update = () => setSize({ width: window.innerWidth, height: window.innerHeight }); update(); window.addEventListener('resize', update); return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', update); }, [isHydrated]); return size; } // --- Hydration Error Boundary --- interface ErrorBoundaryProps { children: ReactNode; fallback?: ReactNode | ((error: Error) => ReactNode); onError?: (error: Error, info: ErrorInfo) => void; } interface ErrorBoundaryState { hasError: boolean; error: Error | null; didFallbackToCSR: boolean; } export class HydrationErrorBoundary extends React.Component< ErrorBoundaryProps, ErrorBoundaryState > { state: ErrorBoundaryState = { hasError: false, error: null, didFallbackToCSR: false, }; static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): Partial { return { hasError: true, error }; } componentDidCatch(error: Error, info: ErrorInfo) { const isHydrationError = error.message.includes('Hydration') || error.message.includes('Text content does not match') || error.message.includes('did not match'); if (isHydrationError) { console.warn( '[Hydration] Mismatch detected, falling back to client-side render:', error.message ); // Force a client-side re-render by clearing the error state this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null, didFallbackToCSR: true }); } this.props.onError?.(error, info); } render() { if (this.state.hasError && this.state.error) { const { fallback } = this.props; if (typeof fallback === 'function') { return fallback(this.state.error); } return fallback ??
